content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of positive integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You should make all its elements equal by performing a sequence of operations.

In one operation, you may choose any contiguous subsequence Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl‚Äã,Al+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äã such that for each iii (l+1‚â§i‚â§rl+1 \le i \le rl+1‚â§i‚â§r), Ai=AlA_i = A_lAi‚Äã=Al‚Äã, i.e. a sequence of identical elements, then choose any integer x<Alx \lt A_lx<Al‚Äã, and for each iii (l‚â§i‚â§rl \le i \le rl‚â§i‚â§r), replace AiA_iAi‚Äã by xxx. The integers xxx chosen in different operations may be different.

Find the smallest number of operations necessary to make all elements of the given sequence equal.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105
Ai‚â§NA_i \le NAi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the smallest number of operations.
